title: NullPointerException in PaymentService at checkout
body:
p99 latency on /checkout has jumped from 800ms to 8.2s over the last 14 minutes. Error rate is up 340%. Stack trace from sampled requests:

java.lang.NullPointerException
  at com.bts.payments.PaymentService.processCharge(PaymentService.java:142)
  at com.bts.payments.PaymentController.charge(PaymentController.java:67)
  at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)

Affected: ~2,400 active checkouts. Region: us-east-1, eu-west-2.

triage.likely_cause: payment-service v4.8.2 guest checkout deploy introduced a null dereference at PaymentService.java:142 where customer.savedPaymentMethod is null for new guest checkout flows
recommended_actions: Page #payments-oncall immediately — P0, all checkout users affected across us-east-1 and eu-west-2
Roll back payment-service to v4.8.1 immediately (rollback_available: true, deployed by tom.bryce@bts-synthetic.example)
Apply null check hotfix at PaymentService.java:142: guard customer.savedPaymentMethod before dereference, fall back to request's paymentMethodToken for guest flows
Verify Stripe status at status.stripe.com to rule out upstream contribution
triage.reasoning: payment-service v4.8.2 (adding guest checkout support) was deployed at 14:04 UTC, and the NPE storm began at 14:18 UTC — a 14-minute gap consistent with a warm traffic ramp. The stack trace at PaymentService.java:142 is the exact line documented in the payment-service-degraded runbook for the known unfixed NPE where customer.savedPaymentMethod is null in guest checkout flows. With ~2,400 active checkouts impacted across both us-east-1 and eu-west-2, this is a global P0 affecting all checkout users. Fastest remediation is rolling back payment-service to v4.8.1; a targeted null-check hotfix at PaymentService.java:142 must follow before re-enabling guest checkout.
